REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT KEITH KNAPP NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RELIEF FROMENTRY OF DEFAULT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. AS TO CCP 473(D) DEFENDANT FAILS TO EXPLAIN HOW A REVIEW OF THE JUDGMENT ROLL, INCLUDING THE PROOF OF SERVICE ON WHICH THE DEFAULT IS BASED, WOULD SHOW THAT AS A MATTER OF LAW (I.E. CITATION TO CASES) THE DEFAULT IS IMPROPER FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION. DEFENDANT QUOTES CCP 415.20 AND PROCEEDS TO CONCLUDE WITHOUT ANY ANALYSIS OF FACT OR LAW THAT SERVICE IN THIS CASE WAS IMPROPER. DEFENDANT FAILED TO CITE ANY CASES FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE EQUITABLE RELIEF IS APPROPRIATE. MCCREADIE V. ARQUES (1967) 248 CAL.APP.2D 39 IS FACTUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE. FINALLY, UNDER CCP 473(B) DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE DEFAULT WAS DUE TO MISTAKE, SURPRISE OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT'S DECLARATION (1) DOES NOT DENY THAT THE ADDRESS OF SERVICE WAS HIS MAILING ADDRESS AT THE RELEVANT TIME; (2) DOES NOT DENY THE RECEIPT OF THE MAILED COPIES OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT; (3) DOES NOT DENY THAT THE ADDRESS AT ISSUE WAS LISTED WITH A PROFESSIONAL LICENSING AUTHORITY AND HAS NOT BEEN CHANGED TO PROVIDE DIFFERENT INFORMATION; (4) DOES NOT DENY THAT HE HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THIS ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2020 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Set For Trial On Mar-23-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2019 Continued To Sep-11-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief Fromentry Of Default
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Reno Fernandez, Esq., 415-362-0449, appearing for Dakota Note, Defendant and moving party.

Thomas Palmer (via courtcall), Esq, 650-692-4001, appearing for Verliant Energy, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Mr. Fernandez stated that his motion suffers from defective notice. Mr. Palmer reported that he had no objection to the court reviewing a declaration.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KEITH KNAPP Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief Fromentry Of Default is DENIED without prejudice. As to CCP 473(d) Defendant fails to explain how a review of the judgment roll, including the Proof of Service on which the default is based, would show that as a matter of law (i.e. citation to cases) the default is improper for lack of jurisdiction. Defendant quotes CCP 415.20 and proceeds to conclude without any analysis of fact or law that service in this case was improper.
Defendant failed to cite any cases for the proposition that under the circumstances of this case equitable relief is appropriate. McCreadie v. Arques (1967) 248 Cal.App.2d 39 is factually distinguishable.
Finally, under CCP 473(b) Defendant failed to show that the default was due to mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. The Court notes that Defendant's declaration (1) does not deny that the address of service was his mailing address at the relevant time; (2) does not deny the receipt of the mailed copies of the summons and complaint; (3) does not deny that the address at issue was listed with a professional licensing authority and has not been changed to provide different information; (4) does not deny that he had knowledge of this action.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel Re First Amended Complaint (C.C.P. 128.7)
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Palmer, Esq. of The Law Office of Mark C. Watson, 650-692-4001, appearing for Verliant Energy, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Reno Fernandez, Esq., and Daniel Vaknin, Esq., 415-362-0449, appearing for Dakota Note, for Defendant and moving party.

Court reporter: Elizabeth J. Lee CSR #5828, telephone: (415)-753-3474, email: lizleecsr@att.net

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) .

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAKOTA NOTE, LLC's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel Re First Amended Complaint (C.C.P. 128.7)is DENIED. Insufficient evidence presented for the Court to conclude that this action was brought or is being pursued in violation of CCP 128.7(b)(1) or (3). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel Re First Amended Complaint (C.C.P. 128.7)
